content: The Self-Refine framework’s iterative, unsupervised self-feedback loop can be repurposed as a foundational backbone to unify scattered LLM self-reflection mechanisms, resolving the limitation that most existing multi-perspective reflection methods require separate auxiliary architecture designs. This unification would allow any off-the-shelf LLM to generate inconsistent, diverse solving perspectives on its own output through repeated Self-Refine iterations, no extra training or labeled data required.
